status_at_import: 13 articles and 5 tools merged via unscramble-enhancements modules.

user_scenarios: A casual player has seven letters and wants to know whether a long play exists.
A student is practicing word-building patterns and wants every word from a small letter set.
A puzzle solver has a blank tile and needs to see how it changes the possible answers.
tips: Start with a higher minimum length when you are hunting for a long answer.
Switch to Scrabble Helper or WWF Helper when score order matters more than length.
Use Word Finder when you know a fixed position, such as C?T or ..ING.
Use the Full list for general vocabulary, not for official game rulings.

internal_links.reason: Use when every letter must be rearranged into a same-length word.
Use when score ranking matters more than length-first browsing.

internal_links.anchor_text: word finder by letters or pattern
internal_links.reason: Use when the user knows positions or wants length-bounded letter search.

internal_links.anchor_text: how to use a word unscrambler
internal_links.reason: Supports users who need a deeper workflow after trying the tool.
Explains how to tame large result sets with length constraints.

schema_suggestions.reason: Describes the browser-based interactive word tool.
Supports the concise step-by-step tool workflow.
Marks up the PAA-style FAQ.
Clarifies the word-tools cluster path.

existing_features: Browser-based rack search using ENABLE1 or a broader dictionary.
Minimum length filter and optional Scrabble/WWF score display.

proposed_upgrades.title: Score blanks as zero and mark which letter each blank represents
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users can trust score estimates more when a blank is involved and can see why a play is possible.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add result caps, pagination, or progressive rendering
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Large racks with blanks stay fast and readable instead of flooding the page.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add include, exclude, starts-with, and ends-with filters
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users can turn a broad rack search into a focused shortlist without switching tools.

proposed_upgrades.title: Explain Game list versus Full list inline
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Players understand why a word appears and which list is safer for casual game use.

user_scenarios: A crossword clue says an answer is an anagram of a given word.
A puzzle solver wants to verify whether LISTEN can become SILENT.
A word-game player wants shorter alternatives from a rack but does not have blanks.
tips: Use All letters first for true anagrams.
Switch to Any letters when no exact word exists or the puzzle allows subsets.
Use Word Finder when you know fixed positions.
Use Word Unscrambler for blank tiles and broader rack-style searches.

internal_links.reason: Use when subsets or blank tiles matter.
Use when the user knows positions or fixed word length.
Teaches human pattern strategies that complement the tool.
Clarifies exact versus subset searches.
Builds topical authority around solving skill.

schema_suggestions.reason: Describes the interactive anagram solver.
Captures exact versus partial mode instructions.
Supports common anagram and phrase-anagram questions.
Places the page in the word-tools cluster.

existing_features: Game list and Full list dictionary modes.
Optional Scrabble and WWF score display.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add optional blank-tile support with clear scoring notes
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Puzzle and game users can solve anagrams that include one or two unknown letters without switching tools.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add phrase-anagram mode as a clearly separate workflow
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users entering multi-word clues can search phrase outputs without confusing the current single-word solver.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add near-anagram mode for one missing or extra letter
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Crossword and spelling-game users can recover from uncertain letters more quickly.

proposed_upgrades.title: Show the sorted-letter key and letter counts
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Learners can understand why duplicate letters matter.

user_scenarios: A solver knows the word is five letters and the third letter is R.
A player has a rack plus one blank and wants only 5 to 7 letter options.
A student is learning how changing one letter creates word families.
tips: Use Pattern mode as soon as you know even one position.
Use dots for unknowns if ? is visually confusing with blank tiles.
In Letters mode, set max length to your rack size or puzzle limit.
Switch to Words Starting/Ending/Containing tools only when the task is pure prefix, suffix, or substring search.

internal_links.reason: Use for broad rack searches without fixed positions.
Use for exact same-letter rearrangements.

internal_links.anchor_text: how to search by word length
internal_links.reason: Explains exact, minimum, and maximum length choices.
Clarifies ? behavior across tools.
Teaches pattern recognition for human solving.

schema_suggestions.reason: Describes the interactive letter and pattern finder.
Captures mode choice and pattern syntax.
Answers wildcard, length, and trust questions.
Supports cluster navigation.

existing_features: Letters mode with rack-fit search and blanks.
Pattern mode with ?, _, and . single-character wildcards.
Min/max length controls in Letters mode.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add must-include and exclude-letter filters
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Wordle and crossword users can remove known-bad letters without leaving the page.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add starts-with, ends-with, and contains fields in the same interface
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users can combine pattern and substring clues instead of bouncing among tools.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add locked-length presets for common puzzle lengths beyond 5, 6, and 7
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Puzzle solvers can start with the right length faster.

proposed_upgrades.title: Show wildcard and fixed-letter counts beside examples
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Beginners understand why a pattern returns few or many results.

user_scenarios: A player has a Q or Z and wants to know whether a short high-value play exists.
A learner wants to study why some balanced racks produce many bingos.
A casual player has one anchor letter available and wants candidates passing through it.
tips: Use Scored mode for decision-making and Basic mode for studying the word list.
Check high-value short words before choosing a long common-letter word.
Subtract blank values manually when a result uses ?.
Pair results with board review because placement can beat raw tile score.

internal_links.reason: Compare WWF scoring and bonus differences.
Use for length-first browsing and broader word discovery.
Supports score strategy beyond the tool UI.

internal_links.anchor_text: using board letters with rack tools
internal_links.reason: Explains how to handle the current one-letter board constraint honestly.
Short-word study supports hook and tight-board play.

schema_suggestions.reason: Describes the interactive Scrabble-style helper.
Captures rack, blank, and board-letter workflow.
Answers dictionary, blank, and scoring questions.
Places the page within word tools.

existing_features: Score-ranked rack results using standard Scrabble tile values.

proposed_upgrades.title: Fix blank scoring to zero with visible blank attribution
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Players can trust displayed scores and understand which tile was played as a blank.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add full-board placement and premium-square scoring
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users can move from candidate words to actual playable moves with realistic scores.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add official dictionary modes where licensing permits
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Tournament and club users can choose a word list that matches their rules.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add rack leave notes for common high-value and vowel-heavy leaves
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Players learn why the best immediate score may not be the best strategic move.

user_scenarios: A WWF player has high-value tiles and wants a ranked shortlist.
A casual player wants to compare the same rack in Scrabble and WWF.
A learner wants to understand why WWF and Scrabble scores differ.
tips: Use WWF Helper for WWF racks and Scrabble Helper for Scrabble racks.
Compare the second-best score and score gap before choosing.
Use short-word lists when a board is tight.
Manually adjust blank-involved scores until blank scoring is fixed.

internal_links.reason: Compare Scrabble tile values and bonus behavior.
Use for broader word discovery not limited to score ranking.
Explains score-sorted thinking across word games.

internal_links.anchor_text: best short words from scrambled letters
internal_links.reason: Supports short-word strategy for tight boards.
Short words are essential for WWF-style tight-board play.

schema_suggestions.reason: Describes the interactive WWF-style helper.
Captures rack, blank, and optional board-letter workflow.
Answers WWF dictionary and scoring questions.
Supports cluster navigation.

proposed_upgrades.title: Fix blank scoring and display blank attribution
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: WWF score estimates become more trustworthy when blanks are involved.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add full WWF board layout and premium-square scoring
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users can evaluate actual playable WWF moves, not just rack candidates.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add a clear WWF dictionary disclaimer panel
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Users understand ENABLE1 proxy behavior before relying on a word.

proposed_upgrades.title: Add side-by-side Scrabble versus WWF score comparison
proposed_upgrades.user_benefit: Players moving between games can see why the best word changes.
